steps.title: Identify what the question is asking
steps.content: The question asks which choice "most effectively uses information from the given sentences to emphasize how Wilkerson documents the Great Migration." The key note is: "To document this period, Wilkerson narrates the personal journeys of Ida Mae Gladney and Robert Pershing Foster, among others." The correct answer must make that documentation method its central point.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and pick the match
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In 2011, Isabel Wilkerson's book *The Warmth of Other Suns* won not only the Mark Lynton History Prize but also the Heartland Prize.': Focuses on award wins, not on how she documents the migration. Doesn't mention narrating individual journeys at all. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Isabel Wilkerson chronicles the complex history of the Great Migration in such riveting, personal detail by narrating the journeys of individuals, such as Ida Mae Gladney and Robert Pershing Foster.': Directly states her documentation method — narrating individual journeys — which is exactly what the question asks to emphasize. ✓
  • Choice C — 'The personal journey of Ida Mae Gladney is featured in the award-winning book *The Warmth of Other Suns*.': Mentions one individual but frames the sentence around the book's award status, not around Wilkerson's documentation strategy. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Denver, Colorado, is one of the cities whose African American populations grew during the Great Migration.': Describes the scope of the Great Migration, not Wilkerson's documentation method at all. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
